ExpTracker.title: EXP / Venture Tracker
ExpTracker.NoPermission: Currently only GMs can use this application.

BabySteps.label: Baby's First Steps
BabySteps.hint: If the Average Level that the Base EXP Reward was calculated from is less then 10, apply this bonus. It is here to help out while you're in those lone digits!

MajorNPCDefeated.label: Major NPC Defeated
MajorNPCDefeated.hint: Such as a Gym Leader, Team Rocket Lieutenant or Rival. Feel free to increase this reward for higher-stakes fights with bosses such as Legendaries, or Elite Four members, or even Giovanni himself!

ThwartedEvilPlans.label: Thwarted Evil Plans
ThwartedEvilPlans.hint:
Think defeating a Pokémon that was terrorizing an area after Team Rocket pissed it off by attacking its young!
Or if you are the evil doers… thwarting the Forces of Good can be just as enticing…

ImpressiveStrategy.label: Impressive Usage of Strategy
ImpressiveStrategy.hint:
This can be anything like the Players using the terrain to their advantage; to impressive teamwork or even just quick thinking on the spot! Award your players for their ingenuity!

Some more examples:

  • Defeating the Kangaskhan by having Scorbunny run around its legs with rope to take it down like an AT-AT
  • Destroy the nearby Water Tower to make everyone Wet and in for a Shocking Surprise.
  • The Stealth Rocks that Skarmory put up made it so that the Charizard couldn’t charge in against it and instead force it to come in close and fight the Seismitoad ready to fight in close-quarters with this Lizard!
  • You managed to allow the entire party to benefit from your Sandstorm strategy; yet they barely even have any Ground, Steel or Rock types!

ImpressedMajorNPC.label: Impressing (Major) NPCs
ImpressedMajorNPC.hint:
You may not have beaten the Elite Four member with your 3 gym badges… But they’re impressed by how good you already are!
You can consider not giving the “Lost Encounter” penalty when awarding this bonus.

EntertainTheCrowd.label: Entertain the Crowd
EntertainTheCrowd.hint: You really know how to give a show! The NPCs watching your fight be it Humans or Pokémon really loved watching that, or are happy and thankful you resolved the situation before it could further get out of hand.

AllySaved.label: Ally Saved
AllySaved.hint: You managed to save a Player-controlled or NPC ally; be it by reducing the damage they would receive by lowering it; making an attack miss; taking the hit yourself, or plainly removing the source of damage! Teamwork is the dreamwork!

SucceedAgainstOdds.label: Succeed against all odds
SucceedAgainstOdds.hint: Succeed on a Skill Check in which you have less than 20 points invested!

DestroyedPublicInfrastructure.label: Destroyed Public Infrastructure
DestroyedPublicInfrastructure.hint:
You better have a good explanation for Officer Jenny…

Don’t award this penalty if players received permission.

HalfPartyFainted.label: Gruesome; half the party or more fainted!

MajorInfluencer.label: Major Influencer
MajorInfluencer.hint: This social encounter involves someone or something key to the plot of this Journey, it’s impressive you managed to survive!

AvoidFight.label: Avoided a Fight
AvoidFight.hint:
Wow that was gonna be scary, glad you talked your way out of it!

Only award this if the Fight was going to be challenging; talking down an angry Caterpie with your Charizard ain’t gonna award you additional EXP.

ConvincingStory.label: Convincing Story
ConvincingStory.hint: Who knows you could convince someone like that! You managed to convince someone of a fact that wasn’t… quite true, but surely it will help you achieve your goal!

VeryPersuasive.label: Very Persuasive
VeryPersuasive.hint: Sway someone to do something they didn’t originally plan to do; or didn’t agree with before, to now help you out / agree with!

WonArgument.label: What an Argument
WonArgument.hint: You just made the GM stutter with how ingenious your argument was, awesome work.

AwesomeRoleplay.label: Awesome Roleplay
AwesomeRoleplay.hint: That was super in-character, absolutely love it!

MadeTheTableLaugh.label: Made the Table Laugh
MadeTheTableLaugh.hint: We’re playing games for fun right? Sometimes a Dad joke is appropriate after all…

QuickWitted.label: Quick Witted
QuickWitted.hint: You handled that situation with ease despite being unprepared, or perhaps your information was incorrect? Either way, you did a great job!

OffendedNPC.label: Offended the NPC
OffendedNPC.hint: You were trying to befriend them… not offend them…

SuccessfulCamp.label: Nice Meal, Cozy Fire, a successful Camp!
SuccessfulCamp.hint: Succeed at more than half of your Camping Checks (min 1).

DoSkillChallenge.label: Participate in a Skill Challenge
DoSkillChallenge.hint: Whether you succeed or lost; you helped move the plot forward! Succeed at a skill challenge set-up by the GM.

WinIntroduction.label: End first in the Introduction / Visual Phase

TrickOpponent.label: Trick Your Opponent
TrickOpponent.hint: Make your opponent Fumble through the usage of a cleverly timed move

TopPerformer.label: Top Performer
TopPerformer.hint: Score the highest amount of appeal in any round of the ‘Secondary Judging’ better known as the ‘Moves Evaluation’

LastPlace.label: Get Fourth Place or Worse Overall

WinAnySection.label: Place First in a section of a Triathlon

Slug.label: Name of list
Slug.hint: For automation purposes, the name of the list should be lower-dash-case and match the slug of the appropriate Trait, Egg List or Ability.

Right click a key to copy path to clipboard,

right click a value to copy that value to clipboard.


The Data Inspector for Chat Messages will show you info about the rolls in those messages

Right click a domain or option to copy that value to clipboard.

priority.label: Priority
priority.hint:
Affliction's are applied in batches based on their priority, from lowest to highest.
The system keeps all healing Affliction at priority 0, and all damaging Afflictions at priority 30, and in-between those numbers special cases are handled.

For homebrew content we recommend using these same numbers if your Affliction is meant to be applied in the same batch as the system Afflictions.

Descriptions.fainted:
When a creature's HP reaches and remains at 0, it is afflicted with Fainted.

A Fainted creature cannot use Actions, does not benefit from Abilities, and is cured of all @Trait[major-affliction], @Trait[minor-affliction], @Trait[pseudo-affliction], and @Trait[stage-change] effects they possess.

Fainted is cured when the afflicted creature's HP is greater than 0.

The creature's @Affliction[weary] count increases by 1 whenever they are cured of Fainted.
Descriptions.burn: The afflicted creature loses a Tick of HP at the end of each of their Activations. The creature has their default ATK Stages reduced by 1 while Burn is active.
Descriptions.poison: The afflicted creature loses a Tick of HP at the end of each of their Activations. The afflicted creature’s default SPATK Stages are reduced by 1 while Poison is active.
Descriptions.blight: At the end of the afflicted creature’s Activation, it loses X Stacks of Blight, where X is 2<sup style='font-size: x-small;'># of Activations passed with Blight</sup> and takes a Tick of HP per Stack lost. The afflicted creature’s default SPATK Stages are reduced by 1 while Blight is active. When a creature receives Blight, it loses the Poison Affliction, with Blight gaining 33% of its remaining Poison Stacks as extra Stacks on Blight. When afflicted with Poisoned X while Blighted, the creature gains +1 to its Blight Stacks.
Descriptions.paralysis: The afflicted creature's SPD is reduced by 50% and also has @Affliction[slowed] while Paralysis is active. Additionally, whenever the creature takes an Action, there is a 25% chance to apply @Affliction[tased]{Tased 2} to themselves.
Descriptions.drowsy: The Power of all of the afflicted creature's attacks is reduced by 33%, and the creature cannot utilize the [Priority X] or [Interrupt X] capabilities of their Actions. The afflicted creature's default SPDEF Stages are reduced by 1 while Drowsy is active.
Descriptions.nightmares: This Affliction can only be applied to Drowsy creatures. The Power of all of the afflicted creature's attacks is reduced by 50%, and the creature cannot utilize the [Priority X] or [Interrupt X] capabilities of their actions. The creature loses a Tick of HP at the end of each of its Activations and has their default SPDEF and ACC Stages reduced by 1 while Nightmares is active. When a creature receives Nightmares, it loses the Drowsy Affliction, and gains 33% of its remaining Drowsy Stacks onto Nightmares, and further Nightmares Stacks cannot be applied.
Descriptions.frozen: The afflicted creature is unable to use actions (either during its Activations or as interrupts/reactions), but takes 33% less damage from all sources. The afflicted creature's default EVA Stages are reduced by 1. Additional Frozen Stacks cannot be added while Frozen is on a creature. When Frozen ticks down to zero Stacks (not cured), the afflicted creature gains Frostbite 5 and their next Activation is Advanced by 50%.
Descriptions.frostbite: The afflicted creature loses a Tick of HP at the end of each of their Activations. The creature has -1 default DEF Stages while Frostbite is active.
Descriptions.splinter: The afflicted creature loses a Tick of HP when they are damaged by a Physical- or Special-Category attack. The creature has Stunted while Splinter is active.
Descriptions.slowed: The afflicted creature’s Movement Scores are reduced by 50%, rounding down (minimum 1).
Descriptions.hindered: The afflicted creature’s Skill DCs are reduced by -10. Hindered applied from different sources have their own Stacks.
Descriptions.stunted: When the afflicted creature recovers HP, that amount is reduced by 2 Ticks (to a minimum of 0 HP healed).
Descriptions.confused: At the end of the afflicted creature's Activations, it has a 50% chance of using Fumble.
Descriptions.hyper-mode:
While afflicted with Hyper Mode, the afflicted creature gains +20% to all of its Movement Scores, while dealing and taking 15% more damage. As well, the user’s Shadow-Type attacks gain [Crit 2], or have their [Crit X] value increase by +2. At the start of the afflicted creature’s Activations, it has:
  • a 40% chance to act normally on that Activation.
  • a 25% chance to be Choice-Locked 1 to Shadow-Type attacks.
  • a 15% chance of ending its Activation and using Fumble.
  • a 15% chance of using Backlash.
  • a 5% chance to be cured of Hyper Mode.
Descriptions.weary: While a creature is Weary, its default ACC and EVA Stages are based on its Weary value, X. Specifically, the default ACC and EVA Stages are set to the negative sum of all X and all integers between 0 and X (Weary 1 sets the defaults to -1, 2 to -3, and 3 to -6). Weary is automatically increased by 1 when a creature Faints. When a creature has 4 or more Weary, it is unable to battle until they reduce their Weary. Weary cannot wear off on its own - a creature must deliberately Rest or consume Items such as Apples to remove Stacks of Weary.
Descriptions.exposed: While a creature is Exposed, it cannot remove Weary.
Descriptions.blinded: The afflicted creature is unable to see, ocularly. The creature has their "seeing-based" vision options disabled (standard vision, Darkvision, X-Ray Vision, etc.) and can only perceive using their other senses (as listed in their Traits). If they do not have a way to see, or have someone guiding them, they are unable to target other creatures with Attacks or Actions.
Descriptions.stuck: While afflicted with Stuck, creatures may not leave the combat area. If the afflicted creature is an owned Pokemon, it cannot willingly switch out (or be switched out by another party). When a creature with the [Trainer] or [Ace] trait would gain Stuck; they instead gain Slowed X, rather than Stuck X.
Descriptions.bound: The afflicted creature cannot willingly use any Movement. Stacks of Bound do not decrease at the end of a creature's Activation, instead the effect that applied Bound will provide a way of removing the Bound Affliction.
Descriptions.drowning: The afflicted creature loses a Tick of HP at the end of each of their Activations. The creature is Suppressed and has -1 default EVA Stage while Drowning is active.
Descriptions.infested: The afflicted creature loses a Tick of HP at the end of each of their Activations. The creature is Hindered and has -1 default DEF Stage while Infested is active.
Descriptions.wind-shear: The afflicted creature loses a Tick of HP at the end of each of their Activations. The creature has -1 default ACC Stage while Wind Shear is active.
Descriptions.tased: The afflicted creature loses a Tick of HP at the end of each of their Activations. The creature has -1 default SPDEF Stage while Tased is active.
Descriptions.wracked: The afflicted creature loses one and a half Ticks of HP at the end of each of their Activations. The creature has -1 default ATK and SPATK Stage while Wracked is active.
Descriptions.disabled: One of the afflicted creature’s attacks cannot be used, under any circumstances. The move in question is determined by the source of the disabled effect, usually being “the last attack the target used.” If this would prevent them from using any attack, they may Struggle instead.
Descriptions.nullified: One of the afflicted creature’s abilities is negated, and cannot be activated or benefited from. The ability in question is determined by the source of the nullification, usually determined randomly.
Descriptions.suppressed: The afflicted creature spends 50% more PP on all actions that use PP, minimum 1.
Descriptions.lifted: The afflicted creature is held aloft. The creature cannot use [Movement] other than [Teleport] while Lifted and is otherwise considered @Affliction[stuck] and @Affliction[raised] for the duration. A Lifted creature also suffers -4 EVA Stages.
Descriptions.raised: While a creature is Raised, it is immune to attacks and effects with the [Earthbound] trait.
Descriptions.destined: When the afflicted creature deals damage with a Physical- or Special-Category Attack to the source of Destined, the afflicted creature is inflicted with the same amount of damage.
Descriptions.braced: while a creature is Braced, its default DEF and SPDEF Stages are increased by +2 and its default ATK and SPATK Stages are decreased by 1. Additionally, a Braced creature is immune to critical damage. A creature loses the Brace Affliction after it Moves or makes an Attack of its own accord.
Descriptions.duel: while a creature is afflicted by Duel, the creature and the source of the Affliction have [Crit 2] on attacks they use against one another, and take and deal 1/3 less damage from/to creatures other than themselves. Creatures afflicted with Duel and the source of the Duel are also Stuck. If either the source or afflicted creature Faint or are otherwise removed from battle, the Duel Affliction is removed.
Descriptions.curled: while Curled, a creature cannot use Held Items and has +1 default DEF Stage. Additionally, moves with the [Curl] Trait have their Power doubled while their user is Curled.
Descriptions.chanted: If an attack were to deal critical damage to a creature with Chanted, the attack instead deals damage as if it were not a Critical Hit.
Descriptions.choice-locked: While Choice-Locked, a creature may only use the last attack or action it used. A creature with the Choice-Locked affliction cannot gain further stacks of Choice-Locked in any way. Choice-Locked ends in the event you are unable to perform the choice-locked action.
Descriptions.grounded: While Grounded, creatures become unable to use their [Flight] movement, nor can they be Lifted or Raised and have those effects removed.
Descriptions.cursed: While Cursed, an afflicted creature loses three ticks of HP at the end of each of their Activations.
Descriptions.true-sight:
The afflicted creature ignores invisibility and illusions.

Additionally, the user's Normal- and Fighting-Type attacks can hit Ghost-Type creatures, and the user's Ghost-Type attacks can hit Normal-Type creatures.
Descriptions.marked: While Marked, all creatures unallied to the afflicted creature and within 8m of it can only target the Marked creature with their attacks. Whenever the creature with Marked is hit by an Attack, reduce the Stack Value of its Marked by 1. A creature that starts or enters the Marked creature's range at any point during their Activation must from that moment on target the Marked creature with any attacks they make, even when leaving the 8m range of the Marked effect.
Descriptions.unlucky: While Unlucky, a creature cannot utilize its Luck.
Descriptions.perish: When a creature’s Perish count hits 0, the creature's HP is set to 0. Humans and ‘Ace’ creatures gain Weary 1 instead of losing HP when their Perish count is 0. The Perish Affliction is removed if a creature is switched out or leaves combat. A creature already suffering from Perish will have its duration shortened by 1 when it would normally gain additional Stacks of Perish.
Descriptions.invisible:
The afflicted creature vanishes from sight, and require a special sense to be targeted by Attacks, as covered by Foundry automation.

Additionally, while Invisible, the creature gains +1 Evasion Stage against AoE Attacks.

Invisible ends after the creature fully resolves an Attack, after an Attack hitting it is fully resolved, by using a Free Action to end Invisible, or by the end conditions of the effect that granted Invisible.

You may always attempt to use an attack on a certain tile to try to hit something you cannot see, when doing so, resolve the attack as a Blind GM Roll with a -1 Accuracy Stage penalty.
Descriptions.transient: The afflicted creature, while still visible, is able to traverse through any material, as if it wasn’t even there. It may use any of its Movements to move through the air or any substance (as if it were always on or in the appropriate substance), ignoring any and all Blocking Terrain. Attacks from unafflicted creatures targeting the user have -40 Accuracy, and afflicted creatures take and deal 75% less damage to/from unafflicted creatures. The afflicted creature loses 1 PP (or gains 1 Weary, if at 0 PP) per Activation while Transient (currently not automated). If the afflicted creature loses Transient while it is an space occupied by a solid material or Blocking Terrain, the creature is forcefully Moved to the nearest open space, taking 2 Ticks of HP per meter Moved.
Descriptions.fear: The afflicted creature does 33% less damage to the source of Fear, and suffers -1 default ACC Stage on attacks against them. Fear applied from different sources have their own Stacks.
Descriptions.leech: The afflicted creature loses two Ticks of HP at the end of each of their Activations and the Source that applied Leech gains HP equal to the amount the afflicted creature lost.
Descriptions.rebound: Damage inflicted on the afflicted creature by Physical- or Special-Category attactks deals ¼ of the inflicted damage on the attacking creature.
Descriptions.ringed: When a creature with Ringed concludes an Activation, they heal a Tick of HP.
Descriptions.bleed: When a creature with Bleed concludes an Activation, they lose a Tick of HP.
Descriptions.enraged: A creature may only use Physical- or Special-Category moves while it is Enraged.
Descriptions.charmed: The afflicted creature believes the source of Infatuation is a dear friend or romantic interest. Afflicted creatures have a 50% chance of being unable to target the source of their infatuation with an attack, and have -2 EVA Stages against attacks from the source of their infatuation.
Descriptions.taunted: The afflicted creature does 33% less damage to creatures other than the source of Taunted. The afflicted creature cannot use Status-category attacks for the duration of Taunted.
Descriptions.grapple:
The afflicted creature possesses -1 default ACC and EVA and may only take Actions with the [Grapple] trait. Creatures that are not in control of a Grapple are also Bound. A creature in control of the Grapple can take a Free Action on their turn to end their participation in Grapple. The creature in control of the Grapple is treated as having the non-in-control creature(s) as being Mounted on itself.
If an afflicted creature is the only participant of a Grapple, they lose this Affliction. Hostile actions made whilst Grappled must target a participant of the Grapple.
Descriptions.boosted: An afflicted creature has the Power of its Physical- and Special-Category Attacks increase by 20%.
Descriptions.resolved: An afflicted creature takes 20% less damage from Physical- and Special-Category Attacks.
Descriptions.loafing:
Automatically applies to creatures 2+ levels above the creature's owner's level. Gaining stacking debuffs for each stack of Loafing.

1 Stack

  • -15% to all Damage
  • -15% to all Skill checks
  • -1 Speed Stage
  • +15% PP Cost

2 Stacks

  • -30% to all Damage
  • -30% to all Skill checks
  • -1 Speed Stage
  • +30% PP Cost
  • -10 Accuracy on all rolls
  • Gain @Affliction[unlucky]

3 Stacks

  • -50% to all Damage
  • -50% to all Skill checks
  • -2 Speed Stage
  • +50% PP Cost
  • -20 Accuracy on all rolls
  • Gain @Affliction[unlucky]
  • -2 to all Movement Scores

4 Stacks

  • -80% to all Damage
  • -80% to all Skill checks
  • -2 Speed Stage
  • +80% PP Cost
  • -30 Accuracy on all rolls
  • Gain @Affliction[unlucky]
  • -5 to all Movement Scores
Descriptions.advance: The afflicted creature's next Activation is Advanced by XY%. Default is 10%.
Descriptions.delay: The afflicted creature's next Activation is Delayed by XY%. Default is 10%.

AvailableAbilities.hint:
Drag & drop abilities onto the Actor sheet or the Available Abilities pop-out window to add them as available options. Then, you drag & drop from the Available Abilities pop-out window into an open Ability Slot to assign abilities to the slots!

Actors gain additional Ability Slots at level 20, 50 and 80. Whenever you gain an Ability from your Species beyond Starting, it must be slotted in these additional Ability Slots. Starting Abilities may be slotted in any slots (meaning, creatures with one Starting Ability will always have that one slotted in the first slot at all times).

Additional Abilities learned through other sources (i.e. via Perks and Tutoring) may only ever be slotted in the 2nd through 4th slots.

luck.label: Luck
luck.hint:
Luck is representative of a "cosmic karma", one that guides a creature's favors and fortunes. Luck allows the manipulation of Skill Checks, and a few other aspects of the game. Luck is a resource that only Player Characters and Aces have, due to the "plot importance" these creatures tend to have, both in-game and out-of-game. At character creation, a Player determines their initial luck by rolling 3d6 and multiplying the result by 5.

For more information, see the Luck section of the Rules, under Skill Checks & Luck.

accounting.label: Accounting
accounting.hint: This skill is used to look over records, be they financial, inventory, or otherwise and look for discrepancies. Things that just don't add up. Serves a niche as an investigation skill, or potentially a professional skill for campaigns that way inclined.

acrobatics.label: Acrobatics
acrobatics.hint: The Acrobatics skill is responsible for jumping and vaulting, along with activities such as zip wiring and parachuting. It does not, however, cover trying to fly at speed but it can be utilized in place of Running for Threaded movement.

appraise.label: Appraise
appraise.hint: This skill is used to determine the relative value of an object. A successful Appraise role might lend an edge as part of a Negotiation, or yield information pertaining to the quality of a piece of gear a character might have.

archaeology.label: Archaeology
archaeology.hint: This skill is used to study ancient things. This includes studying ruins, knowledge of past civilizations, and deciphering lost inscriptions.

aura-sense.label: Aura Sense
aura-sense.hint: The Aura Sense skill governs interactions with Aura, ranging from perceiving and analyzing aura to manipulating it and the process of Channeling.

climb.label: Climb
climb.hint: This is a fairly straightforward skill. It governs the pace and aptitude for climbing, and it what creatures use to Rush while burrowing.

computers.label: Computers
computers.hint: The all-around skill that governs the use of computers and the internet. GMs may find this skill warrants converting into a skill category for more tech-intensive campaigns.

conversation.label: Conversation
conversation.hint: This is a general-purpose social skill that covers interaction uses such as charming and persuading. Any socialization that doesn't cover lying, intimidation or deal-making. In the unusual event that this roll needs to be opposed, the other side should also use Conversation.

disguise.label: Disguise
disguise.hint: For when you need to pretend to be someone or something else. Opposed by Psychology or Spot, depending on which is more appropriate.

electronics.label: Electronics
electronics.hint: This skill is used to understand electronics and perform electrical repairs on devices. Some Pokemon may use the Electronics skill to manipulate or disrupt electrical devices.

engineering.label: Engineering
engineering.hint: This skill is used to understand complex engineering designs. If appropriate can be used in design and construction capacities, but more commonly it would be utilized by a character seeking to sabotage or damage a structure or vehicle.

fast-talk.label: Fast Talk
fast-talk.hint: The skill used to bamboozle, confound, and lie your way around obstacles. Obfuscate your intent with either masterful ad-lib deception, or by merely confusing your opponent.

flying.label: Flying
flying.hint: The Flying skill is used to Rush for Flying Movement, governing a creature's capacity for self-induced flight.

handiwork.label: Handiwork
handiwork.hint: The Handiwork skill covers various trades and crafts, mainly covering construction, woodworking, sewing, fletching, plumbing, smithing, and more! This ranges from broad knowledge of specific trades to specific capability in applying said knowledge, like in terms of Crafting.

history.label: History
history.hint: This skill is used to establish familiarity with historical events. It tends to be more applicable to more recent history, as the ancient falls within the purview of the Archaeology skill.

husbandry.label: Husbandry
husbandry.hint: The function of this skill is to feed and care for others. This skill governs cooking, breeding and general care of both Pokemon and humans.

intimidate.label: Intimidate
intimidate.hint: The Intimidate skill is what you need when you want to scare someone or bully your way into getting what you want. It may get you results, but creatures will likely remember you and it will not be fondly. This skill can be opposed with Intimidate or Leadership.

leadership.label: Leadership
leadership.hint: This skill is used to establish authority over others similar to Intimidate, however with respect rather than fear. It can be used to inspire and command, and interacts with Pack Mon perks. You may use the Leadership skill to bolster an ally that someone is attempting to intimidate.

legal.label: Legal
legal.hint: This skill represents a character's understanding of the legal system and its processes, and can either utilize that knowledge to help stay out of trouble or to have the knowledge needed to manipulate procedures to their advantage.

lift.label: Lift
lift.hint: This skill governs the raw physical ability to lift and carry and to perform feats of strength beyond what a creature could normally do comfortably.

listen.label: Listen
listen.hint: This skill governs the ability to detect creatures or activity through hearing.

locksmith.label: Locksmith
locksmith.hint: The art of picking locks. This can be used as a general breaking & entering skill, with computers governing hacking one's way into electronic systems. In suitably advanced settings, consider allowing Locksmith to work with electronic locks also.

mechanics.label: Mechanics
mechanics.hint: This skill is to perform mechanical repairs and upgrades to machines or devices and working with simpler electrical components. These can range from repairing small gadgets, like flashlights, household appliances, traditional motor vehicles, factory machines, etc., but will likely not cover "smart" devices, like high-end smart phones, non-mechancal computers, or cyberpunk/sci-fi type "bleeding edge" devices and implements in more contemporary campaign settings.

medicine.label: Medicine
medicine.hint: This skill governs all medical training, whether that be diagnostics, first aid or surgery.

natural-world.label: Natural World
natural-world.hint: The Natural World skill is used for a character to understand the fundamentals of nature, such as identifying trees and mosses and types of berries and mushrooms. Also covers non-artistic gardening pursuits.

navigate.label: Navigate
navigate.hint: This skill is used to find your way when traveling outside of your normal default habitat. This is as true for migratory Pokemon as it is for humans leaving their cities, but would not be required for a Pokemon staying in its forest, or the human navigating around the city in which they live.

negotiation.label: Negotiation
negotiation.hint: This skill covers the art of the deal. If you want something and the other creature is willing to give you it, for a price, then this skill can be used to determine who gets the better end of the deal.

profession-specific.label: Profession (Specific)
profession-specific.hint: This skill represents the user's skill and experience in a specific line of work (businessman, chef, teacher, etc.).

psychology.label: Psychology
psychology.hint: This skill is used to determine someone's mental or emotional state. This can either be from a perspective of reading their intentions, providing them with treatment (such as traumatized Shadow Pokemon), or seeing through someone's Disguise act or Fast Talking.

read-lips.label: Read Lips
read-lips.hint: This skill is used when a character is able to see someone speaking but is unable to hear them for whatever reason, and wants to understand what they are saying. Potentially useful for any hearing-impaired characters.

research.label: Research
research.hint: This skill is used to hit the books at the library or similar. Specifically for finding out information that your character needs but does not already know by virtue of their other skills.

resources.label: Resources
resources.hint: This skill cannot be raised using skill points after level 1. The Resources skill is used to determine your character's spending power, and can also serve as a measure of their status.

ride.label: Ride
ride.hint: The Ride skill is used to stay mounted on another creature. For gentle transit, Ride checks generally should not be required, but once things get tense a Ride skill can be all that separates you from a mouthful of dirt. May also be used in riding competitions.

running.label: Running
running.hint: The Running skill is for when overland creatures gotta go fast. Use this skill to Rush using Overland Movement, or to participate in feats of speed during events such as a Pokeathlon.

sleight-of-hand.label: Sleight of Hand
sleight-of-hand.hint: This skill covers feats of legerdemain and physical misdirection, such as card tricks and other non-magical illusions along with the likes of picking pockets.

spot.label: Spot
spot.hint: As the name implies, this skill is utilized to spot things, usually something hard to see or outright hidden. That can be creatures within your field of view trying to use Stealth against you, or it can be used in an investigative context to look for hidden clues.

stealth.label: Stealth
stealth.hint: This skill covers efforts to conceal yourself that do not rely on disguise or social interaction. This is opposed by Listen where creatures do not have you in their field of view, or Spot if they do. Can also be utilized to blend into crowds and to tail unsuspecting marks.

survival.label: Survival
survival.hint: This skill is used to find shelter and to seek out edible foods in the wilderness. It may be appropriate to use for field crafting of things like rafts and temporary bridges, especially if you do not have a full handyman's or mechanic's toolset to tackle normally.

swim.label: Swim
swim.hint: The Swim skill covers all applications of the Acrobatics and Running skills but in the water, including using Rush. It also covers any checks that might be required in line with diving.

teaching.label: Teaching
teaching.hint: This skill is used to help instruct another creature in a skill or other knowledge you already possess. Used for more general Aid rolls and all varieties of Tutoring.

track.label: Track
track.hint: The Track skill is used to follow the trail that another creature has left in order to follow them to their home or other location. If the creature is actively trying to avoid being followed, this is opposed by Stealth. Also used for Fishing.

arts.label: Arts
arts.hint: This is a collection of undefined skills for the most part, though some examples might exist such as Painting or Flower Arrangement. This skill is a measure of talent for creative pursuits and is left to the player to define the bounds of their art.

painting.label: Painting
painting.hint: This skill is used to create works of art using paint. This can be used to create paintings, murals, or other works of art.

sculpting.label: Sculpting
sculpting.hint: This skill is used to create works of art using clay or stone. This can be used to create sculptures, statues, or other works of art.

acting.label: Acting
acting.hint: This skill is used to perform on stage or in front of a camera. This can be used to act in plays, movies, or other performances.

dancing.label: Dancing
dancing.hint: This skill is used to perform dance routines. This can be used to dance in shows, competitions, or other performances.

singing.label: Singing
singing.hint: This skill is used to perform songs. This can be used to sing in concerts, competitions, or other performances.

flower-arrangement.label: Flower Arrangement
flower-arrangement.hint: This skill is used to create works of art using flowers. This can be used to create bouquets, wreaths, or other works of art.

writing.label: Writing
writing.hint: This skill is used to create written works. This can be used to write books, articles, or other written works.

occult.label: Occult
occult.hint: The Occult skills are actually several skills under one group, purchased separately. These are Psychic, Ghost, Dragon, Fairy, Spiritual, Legendary, Paradox. The skills cover knowledge about the topics they’re named for. GMs may wish to add more Occult knowledge as appropriate for their campaign setting.

psychic.label: Psychic
psychic.hint: This skill is used to understand and use psychic powers, including using Rush while utilizing Teleport Movement.

ghost.label: Ghost
ghost.hint: This skill is used to understand and interact with ghostly entities.

dragon.label: Dragon
dragon.hint: This skill is used to understand and interact with dragon-like entities.

fairy.label: Fairy
fairy.hint: This skill is used to understand and interact with fairy-like entities.

spiritual.label: Spiritual
spiritual.hint: This skill is used to understand and interact with spiritual entities.

legendary.label: Legendary
legendary.hint: This skill is used to understand and interact with legendary entities.

paradox.label: Paradox
paradox.hint: This skill is used to understand and interact with paradoxical entities.

alchemy.label: Alchemy
alchemy.hint: Alchemy governs the rigorous, practical approach toward bridging the gap between convential science and supernatural phenomena. Usually focused on materials science, the foundational knowledge of alchemy generally requires a solid background in a number of other sciences and occult disciplines.

thaumaturgy.label: Thaumaturgy
thaumaturgy.hint: Thaumaturgy is the knowledge of the deeper magical mysteries - wisdom, theory, and speculation. Thaumaturgy is the purest, but the most dangerous, approach to learning and wielding magic. Mages must balance their own hubris with the great temptations beyond the veil.

performance.label: Performance
performance.hint: Performance comes in Cool, Clever, Cute, Beauty and Tough variations and these are the primary skills used for Pokemon Contests, but that doesn’t need to be their only applications. We encourage you to let your creativity thrive with these.

cool.label: Cool
cool.hint: This skill is used to perform cool tricks or stunts. This can be used to impress an audience, intimidate an opponent, or show off in general.

clever.label: Clever
clever.hint: This skill is used to perform clever tricks or stunts. This can be used to outsmart an opponent, solve a puzzle, or show off in general.

cute.label: Cute
cute.hint: This skill is used to perform cute tricks or stunts. This can be used to charm an audience, distract an opponent, or show off in general.

beauty.label: Beauty
beauty.hint: This skill is used to perform beautiful tricks or stunts. This can be used to impress an audience, inspire an opponent, or show off in general.

tough.label: Tough
tough.hint: This skill is used to perform tough tricks or stunts. This can be used to intimidate an audience, overpower an opponent, or show off in general.

pilot.label: Pilot
pilot.hint: This group of skills, purchased separately, measure a character’s ability to pilot a vehicle. The example Piloting skills we have included here are Bike, Small Motor Vehicles, Cars, Utility Vehicles, Military Ground Vehicles, Walkers, Aircraft, Aerospace Vehicles, Exotic Vehicle (Specific), Watercraft. You may wish to add or remove from this list as appropriate for your campaign setting (such as carriages in a fantasy setting.)

bike.label: Bike
bike.hint: This skill is used to pilot a bike.

small-motor-vehicles.label: Small Motor Vehicles
small-motor-vehicles.hint: This skill is used to pilot small motor vehicles.

cars.label: Cars
cars.hint: This skill is used to pilot cars.

utility-vehicles.label: Utility Vehicles
utility-vehicles.hint: This skill is used to pilot utility vehicles.

military-ground-vehicles.label: Military Ground Vehicles
military-ground-vehicles.hint: This skill is used to pilot military ground vehicles.

walkers.label: Walkers
walkers.hint: This skill is used to pilot walkers.

aircraft.label: Aircraft
aircraft.hint: This skill is used to pilot aircraft.

aerospace-vehicles.label: Aerospace Vehicles
aerospace-vehicles.hint: This skill is used to pilot aerospace vehicles.

exotic-vehicle-specific.label: Exotic Vehicle (Specific)
exotic-vehicle-specific.hint: This skill is used to pilot a specific exotic vehicle.

watercraft.label: Watercraft
watercraft.hint: This skill is used to pilot watercraft.

science.label: Science
science.hint: The Science group skill covers a number of fields, purchased separately. Examples include Astronomy, Biology, Botany, Chemistry, Cryptography, Forensics, Geology, Mathematics, Meteorology, Parapsychology, Pharmacy, Physics, Zoology. Pokémon-specific fields include Megalobiology, Terastology, Ultrology, Paradoxian Studies. The GM may add to or remove from this list as appropriate to their campaign. This skill represents specialized knowledge in a field and will generally rely upon either specific perks, or a situational application that the GM or Player must call for.

astronomy.label: Astronomy
astronomy.hint: This skill is used to study the stars and planets.

biology.label: Biology
biology.hint: This skill is used to study living organisms.

botany.label: Botany
botany.hint: This skill is used to study plants.

chemistry.label: Chemistry
chemistry.hint: This skill is used to study chemicals.

cryptography.label: Cryptography
cryptography.hint: This skill is used to study codes and ciphers.

forensics.label: Forensics
forensics.hint: This skill is used to study evidence from crime scenes.

geology.label: Geology
geology.hint: This skill is used to study rocks and minerals.

mathematics.label: Mathematics
mathematics.hint: This skill is used to study numbers and equations.

meteorology.label: Meteorology
meteorology.hint: This skill is used to study the weather.

palaeontology.label: Palaeontology
palaeontology.hint: This skill is used to study ancient organisms and fossils.

parapsychology.label: Parapsychology
parapsychology.hint: This skill is used to study psychic phenomena, and for using Rush while utilizing Teleport Movement.

pharmacy.label: Pharmacy
pharmacy.hint: This skill is used to study drugs and medicines.

physics.label: Physics
physics.hint: This skill is used to study matter and energy.

zoology.label: Zoology
zoology.hint: This skill is used to study animals.

eschatobiology.label: Eschatobiology
eschatobiology.hint: This skill is used to study the biology and mechanics governing Mega Evolution.

megalobiology.label: Megalobiology
megalobiology.hint: This skill is used to study the Dynamax Phenomenon.

terastology.label: Terastology
terastology.hint: This skill is used to study the Terastal Phenomenon.

ultrology.label: Ultrology
ultrology.hint: This skill is used to study the biology of Ultra Beasts.

paradoxian-studies.label: Paradoxian Studies
paradoxian-studies.hint: This skill is used to study the biology of Paradox Pokemon.

welcome.title: Welcome to the PTR2e Beta Test!
welcome.description: This is a guided tour of the system to help you get started.

welcome.title: Beta Test
welcome.content:
Since you are playing a Beta of PTR2e, you are likely to run into Foundry Bugs and likely even find certain aspects of the rules that aren't quite fully fleshed out, or may not be quite as balanced as we would like!
That said, we have been in Closed Alpha for about a year now, and have run multiple successfull campaigns in that period of time, so we expect most things to go quite smoothly!
Please be sure to provide feedback whenever you can, now is the time to get your suggestions in!

discord.title: Discord
discord.content: The Discord is the primary place for communication and support for the system. You can find the Discord here.

feedback.title: Reporting Bugs, providing Feedback and Suggestions!
feedback.content:
If you ever run into a bug, or have feedback or suggestions, please let us know! There are a couple ways you can do so:
  • On the discord post in #2e-feedback
  • Use one of the following commands on the Discord Server:
    • /create-issue 2e
    • /bug-report 2e
    • /feature-request 2e
  • Directly report things over on the Github

rules.title: Finding the Rules
rules.content: The rules can both be found on the PTR2e Wiki as well as in The Rules journal here within Foundry!.

style.title: Style Tip!
style.content: Foundry has added build in support for Light vs Dark mode, and we're making use of this! Please check out the core Foundry Settings to set your preferred mode!

next.title: Next Steps
next.content:
We've included a bunch of Tours for you to use to learn more about the Foundry side of things.
If you ever need to check them out again, you can find them in Tour Management in the Settings Tab of the Sidebar.

folders.title: Folders
folders.description: Learn more about the PTR2e Unique Team & Party Folders. There to upgrade your organisational needs!

welcome.title: Welcome to the Folders Tour!
welcome.content:
This tour will guide you through the Team and Party Folders, unique to PTR2e!
Lets get started with creating a New Folder!

createDialog.title: Creating a New Folder
createDialog.content:
While creating a Folder you can set up either a Team or a Party by dragging any Actor to the appropriate field.
Teams are a collection of Actors, for example all your PCs or perhaps all the NPCs in a specific location.
Parties meanwhile are for a Trainer or Expedition Leader and their Pokémon.

createParty.title: Creating a Party
createParty.content:
Dragging in an Actor onto the 'Owner' field will create a Party Folder for that actor, as can be seen here for Tour-san!
Party Folders automatically display all their associated Actors within them once they've been created
This includes the owner and their direct Party Members
Any other related Actors can be added in any (regular) Sub Folder, for example a 'Box' Folder for all the Pokémon in a PC's Box.

openPartySheet.title: Opening a Party Sheet
openPartySheet.content:
You can open a Party Sheet by clicking the 'Open Party Sheet' icon on a Party Folder.
This will show you all the details of the Party.

openedPartySheet.title: Opened Party Sheet
openedPartySheet.content:
Here you can see the Party Sheet for Tour-san, including all their Party Members.
In the 'Organize Party/Boxes' Tab you can see all the Actors owned by the Owner, and manage them from here.

organizeParty.title: Organize party
organizeParty.content:
Simply by Dragging & Dropping actors between the Party & Boxes sections you can easily manage your Party Members and Boxed Pokémon.
Tip: You can drag an entire box into the party to swap the contents of that box with the party!

addPartyMembers.title: Adding Party Members
addPartyMembers.content: You can add Party Members to a Party Folder by dragging in any Actor on top of the folder or it's 'Party' section in the Sidebar's Actor Tab. This will automatically add them to the Party Folder.

createTeam.title: Creating a Team
createTeam.content:
Dragging in an Actor onto the 'Team Members' field will create a Team Folder including that Actor, as you can see here for Tour-san and their Voltorb!
This won't directly move these actors into this folder, allowing you to keep track of any related Actors, like all PCs or all the NPCs in a specific location, without moving them around.

openTeamSheet.title: Opening a Team Sheet
openTeamSheet.content:
You can open a Team Sheet by clicking the 'Open Team Sheet' icon on a Team Folder.
This will show you all the details of the Team, including all the Team Members.

openedTeamSheet.title: Opened Team Sheet
openedTeamSheet.content:
Here you can see the Team Sheet of which Tour-san and their Voltorb are part of.
In the Team's Parties Tab you can see all the Parties owned by Team Members, and manage them from here. (Coming Soon!)

actorSheet.title: Actor Sheet Walkthrough
actorSheet.description: A walkthrough of the Character Sheet, to help you get started with making your first character!

welcome.title: Welcome to the Actor Sheet Walkthrough!
welcome.content:
This tour will guide you through the Actor Sheet, where you can see all the details of your Characters and NPCs!
Let's get started with the Character Overview!

overview.title: Character Overview
overview.content:
On the overview tab you can see some basic details of your Character.
Such as your type effectiveness chart, favourited skills list, movement speeds, and stats.

favouriteSkills.title: Favourite Skills
favouriteSkills.content:
Any skills marked as Favourite will show up in the favourite list, for quick access and reference!
Favourite skills also show in the Token Panel's 'Skills' Tab!

movement.title: Movement
movement.content:
You can see your Character's Movement Speeds in the Movement Section.
At this point in time there is no automation for tracking speed, and so you will have to track this manually until it is added in a later build!

stats.title: Stats
stats.content: Over here you can see your stats radar, you can open up the editor by double clicking on the radar!

statsEditor.title: Stats Editor
statsEditor.content:
In the Stats Editor you can adjust your stats, and see how they affect your derived stats!
Humanoids will be able to assign both their Base Stats and EVs, while Pokémon can only update their EVs.

sidebar.title: Sidebar
sidebar.content:
The Sidebar is available in every tab and shows you some basic details like your HP, PP, EXP and Combat Stages.
You can also access the Perk Web from here!

actions.title: Actions
actions.content:
The Actions Tab is where you can find all the different Actions your Character can take.
This includes Attack Actions, Passives, Generic Actions, and more.

attacks.title: Attack Actions
attacks.content: Every Character can know an unlimited amount of Attacks! However, they may only have a maximum of 6 Attacks occuping their Attack Slots, which can be changed whenever you Camp through the 'Known Attacks' menu.

knownAttacks.title: Known Attacks
knownAttacks.content:
From here you can simply drag & drop the Attacks you want to use today into the right slot on the Actor Sheet, as well as manage which Attacks you know in general.
'Free' Attacks, those that do not take up an Attack Slot such as Fling, are not shown here.

otherAttacks.title: Other Attacks
otherAttacks.content: Any Attack that doesn't take up an Attack Slot, shows up here.

genericActions.title: Generic Actions
genericActions.content: Besides the Combat Actions tab there are also the Downtime Actions and Generic Actions tab, some of these may be used in combat, but most are for out of combat use.

inventory.title: Inventory
inventory.content:
The Inventory Tab is where you can see all the Items your Character is carrying.
This includes all the Items in their Backpack, as well as any Items they are Wearing or Holding.
Currently the inventory has an extremely basic implementation, and the appropriate sections will show up once you add items of their types to your sheet.

inventoryPoints.title: Inventory Points
inventoryPoints.content:
You can see how many Inventory Points your Character has in the Inventory Points Section and track them!
For now, you will have to track these manually until we add automation for this in a later build!

skills.title: Skills
skills.content:
The Skills Tab is where you can see all the Skills your Character knows.
Skills can effectively be in 3 states, 'Favourited', 'Normal' and 'Hidden'.
Favourite skills show at the top of the Skills list, as well as showing up in the 'Favourite Skills' component and Token Panel's 'Skills' tab.
Hidden Skills meanwhile aren't shown, you can use this to hide skills that aren't important to your settings, or that you doubt your character will ever use!
You can always unhide them using the 'Toggle Hidden Skills' button on the right-hand side.

popoutSkills.title: Popout Skills
popoutSkills.content:
A lot of components can be 'Popped Out', just like the Skills List!
This allows you to have multiple components that are on different tabs open at once, or even exclusively open a component in a new window and be able to close the Character Sheet!
You can do this by simply clicking the 'Popout' Icon in the top right corner of any component that supports this.

editSkills.title: Edit Skills
editSkills.content:
Whenever you have skill points to spend it'll be quite obvious by the glowing 'Open Skills Editor' button!
Click it to open the Skills Editor and spend your points!

perks.title: Perks
perks.content: The Perks Tab is where you can see all the Perks your Character has, as well as any possible Abilities.

traits.title: Traits
traits.content:
Besides Perks & Abilities, you can also view any Traits your actor has here.
You can manually add more traits here simply by clicking on the bar and typing the required trait in.
Traits are currently partially automated, for example any Traits from your Species will be added here. But any traits granted by Perks or other sources will have to be added manually for the foreseeable future.

effects.title: Effects
effects.content:
The Effects Tab is where you can see all the Effects your Character has.
Like poor Tour-san here who is Fainted.
Adding effects can be done either through Automation, through dragging in an Effect from the Foundry Sidebar or another Sheet, or through the right-click token-status menu.
Please note that currently the right-click token status menu shows *all* Afflictions in the system, this will later be streamlined to only show those that are pertinent like Fainted, Paralyzed, etc.

perkWeb.title: Perk Web
perkWeb.description: The Perk Web is how you can see and purchase Perks for your Player Characters and NPCs! This tour will guide you on how to use it!

welcome.title: Welcome to the Perk Web Tour!
welcome.content: This tour will guide you through the Perk Web, where you can see all the Perks available to your Character!

rules.title: Perk Rules Overview
rules.content:
Perks are purchased using 'Advancement Points' which are gained through leveling up, and can be spent on any Perk that you meet the prerequisites for.
Besides any prerequisite you may need, every Perk can only be picked up if you have a different perk that connects to it on the Web!
That's why the very first perk you will pick up will be one of the 6 'Root' perks, these perks are special in the sense that you can always pick them up, even with no connection, and the first one is free!

open-web.title: Open the Perk Web
open-web.content:
On the Actor Sheet, in the bottom left corner, you will find the button to open the Perk Web.
Whenever you gain new Advancement Points from level-up, the button will glow to indicate you have Perk Points to spend!

web-overview.title: Perk Web Overview
web-overview.content:
The Perk Web consists of 3 parts, the left sidebar shows you information about your character as well as any info about a perk you have selected.
The center shows you the Perk Web itself
Finally, the right sidebar allows you to search for perks and filter them.
Lets quickly cover the left sidebar!

hud-header.title: Header Bar
hud-header.content:
The header bar has 3 important functions, the left most button allows you to refresh the Perk Web's data, in case you or your GM makes any changes to your Actor or a Perk on the web; so you can see the changes reflected!
Besides that, on the right end, you can find the perk web close button! Very useful, lest you get stuck in here forever!

hud-header-webs.title: Web Selector
hud-header-webs.content: Finally, the select bar in the center allows you to swap to different webs unique to your Actor, for example, a Species' Personal Perk Web, which will also show their Evolution Perks!

hud-actor.title: Actor Info
hud-actor.content: Right below we can find the Actor Info, showing you how many Advancement Points you currently have available to spend!

actor-sheet.title: Actor Sheet
actor-sheet.content:
While you're in the Web, you can open any Foundry Sheet like normal, your Actor Sheet will always show up in minimized in th top left corner.
If you have a module such as Quick Insert installed, you can also search for any Actor/Item/etc. to open it on-top of the Perk Web View.

web-controls.title: Controls
web-controls.content:
You can move the web either by using the scroll bars on the right and bottom, using the [Scroll Wheel] (while holding [Shift] for Horizontal scroll), or by dragging the web around while holding [Right Click].
You can zoom in and out using [Left Click]/[Right Click], or by using the Zoom Level Dropdown on the left sidebar.
To view detailed information on a Perk, you can [Left Click] it to reveal its details.
Hovering over a Perk will show you its Tooltip overview.

perk-overview.title: Perk Overview
perk-overview.content:
I've gone ahead and selected 'Root 1' for you, as you can see over on the left, it's a very simple perk, it's even free!
You can easily buy a perk by clicking the purchasing button right below the perk's details, or, if you already own the perk, you will find that the button has changed to a 'Refund' button!

Please Note: As of Beta 1, there is no automation to check whether a Refund is a 'Valid' Refund. This means that you by refunding a perk you could invalidate other perks, please make sure to only refund perks with the permission of your GM and after making sure it doesn't break any connections!

perk-styling.title: Perk Colors & Styling
perk-styling.content:
A Perk's availability can quickly be glanced by looking at its border color & style, go over each option:
  • Purchased: These perks can easily be identified by their thick blue border, and are the perks you have already obtained!
  • Available: These perks can be identified by their Green Border & White Glow, these are perks you have a valid connection to, meet all the Prerequisites for *and* have enough Advancement Points to purchase!
  • Missing Prerequisite: These perks are easily identified by their Dashed Orange Borders. These are perks that you currently have access to, but do not meet all the Prerequisites for, or do not have enough Advancement Points for. Check their details to figure out what you're missing!
  • Skipped Perk: Sometimes you may find a perk with a Dark Blue/Purple Border, these are perks that are 'Auto Purchased' or 'Skipped' for your Character, for example, the perk Inner Warmth gives your Fire type the [Heater] trait, however, your Torchic already gets this trait from their Species! Thus, while you haven't purchased this perks with your Advancement Points, it counts as having been purchased since Torchic already has the [Heater] trait.
  • Tiered Perk: Another uncommon perk collor is Purple Bordered perks, these are perks that have multiple tiers and can thus be purchased multiple times!
  • Invalid Connection: Perks that your actor owns, but do not have a Valid Connection can be identified by their Red Dashed Borders & Glow. These Perks lack a valid connection leading back to a Root, and thus are 'Invalid', if you ever find yourself with an Invalid Perk, please resolve the issue by fixing purchasing its connections; or discuss with your GM how to fix the situation. A build with an 'Invalid' perk would be considered 'cheating'.
  • Unavailable Perk: Last but not least, unavailable perks which can be identified by their Dashed Gray Borders & Greyed out icon. These perks simply don't have any connection to your currently picked up perks, so you have to work towards them if you wish to unlock them!

perk-example.title: Example
perk-example.content: As an example, Tour-san has purchased Root 1, which is connected to these two perks here, Tactician & Mobilize. Tactician requires us to have at least 30 in the Leadership Skill, meanwhile Mobilize only requires 20. As Tour-san only has 25, Mobilize is currently available, but Tactician is not, as indicated by their Green & Orange Borders respectively.

search.title: Search & Filter
search.content:
Over on the right-hand side, you can Search & Filter for Perks, and makes use of the Compendium Browser's Perk Tab. By default, the search won't load any perks to keep the web snappy, but you can easily load them in by clicking the 'Load Perk Search' button. After which, you can search for any perk you may want!
Clicking on a perk will make the Web pan to that perk, and show you its details! Sometimes Perks are on the web multiple times, clicking on the same perk again will cycle through all the locations it can be found in!

characterCreation.title: Character Creation
characterCreation.description: An interactive walkthrough of the Character Creation process, to help you get started with making your first character!

welcome.title: Unfinished Tour
welcome.content: This tour is coming in a future build!

generatingPokemon.title: Generating Pokémon & How to use Blueprints
generatingPokemon.description: A Walkthrough on 'Blueprints' and the 'Dex Drag-In' system, a returning fan-favourite feature from 1e for quickly generating Pokémon!

welcome.title: Welcome to the Pokémon Generation Tour!
welcome.content: This is a short tour to grant you one of the most powerful tools in the PTR2e system, the 'Dex Drag-In' & Blueprint system!

dex-drag-in.title: Dex Drag-In
dex-drag-in.content: It's incredibly simple, to generate a Pokemon you can do so by dragging any species item, whether that's from the world, the compendium (browser), or what we would recommed; using the module Quick Insert, onto the Canvas! (your Scene) This will open up the Blueprint screen, where you can change certain generation settings.

example.title: Example
example.content:
For example, when I drag Bulbasaur's species item onto the Canvas, this screen would be shown!
Allowing you to set properties such as the Level or Level Range of the generated mon.
Once you've set all the options you want, you simply hit 'Generate Actor(s)'!

blueprints.title: Using Blueprints
blueprints.content:
Blueprints can also be created as Items of their own, at which point they allow creating any range of creatures, from single specialized NPCs, to a Trainer & their party, whether those members are set-in-stone, or even from a random table!
This is also one of the easiest way to create a Trainer & their Party and share it with the Community!
You can Import/Export Blueprints through Foundry's regular way of doing so: right clicking the Item in the Item Tab and selecting the appropriate option.

compendiumBrowser.title: Finding your items in the Compendium Browser
compendiumBrowser.description: A guide on how to use the Compendium Browser to find all the items you need!

welcome.title: Welcome to the Compendium Browser Tour!
welcome.content: The Compendium Browser is a useful tool that allows you to look for & filter data more easily then through the regular Foundry interface.

browser-open.title: Open Compendium Browser
browser-open.content: Step 1 is opening up the Compendium Browser, the button to do so can be found in the 'Compendiums' tab of the Sidebar.

browser-opened.title: Initial Load
browser-opened.content:
Whenever you open the Compendium Browser, it will show you a blank screen, as to avoid loading unnecessary data by default.
To continue you simply click one of the item-type options at the top.

moves-tab.title: Moves Tab
moves-tab.content:
For example, once we have opened the Moves Tab we'll get a list of Moves in the System. You can filter these on the left-hand side.
Useful to know is that you can hover over items in the Compendium Browser like you can in Actor Sheets to view their tooltip preview!

filtering.title: Example: Filter for 'Dash' Moves
filtering.content:
Using the 'Traits' filter, we can for example search for all moves that contain the 'Dash' Trait.
You can combine any amount of filters to find exactly what you're looking for!

automation.title: Effects Automation & The Data Inspector
automation.description: A technical guide on how to use the Data Inspector to automate effects, and how to use the Automation System!

welcome.title: Welcome to the Automation Tour!
welcome.content: The Automation Tour will give you a quick overview of where in the UI to find the automation tools, for an in-depth technical guide you will want to check out the Wiki.

effects.title: Active Effect Automation
effects.content:
PTR2e uses a heavily upgraded variant of the Foundry Active Effect system to automate the effects of basically any item, whether that's a move, ability, perk or otherwise. If you are familiar with PTR 1e or the PF2e system, you will find that the automation system is very compatible to the 'Rules based Automation' of those two systems.
An effect can be added to any type of item, including the 'Effect' item, which can be useful to store effects you may wish to temporarily include on Actors, such as the '+1 Attack Stages' effect item.

effects-tab.title: Effects Tab
effects-tab.content: In the effects tab of any Item/Actor you will see which effects are currently active, and on items you will be able to create new ones with the [+] button at the top right corner.

create-effect.title: Create Effect Dialog
create-effect.content:
There are two types of Active Effect, Afflictions & Passive Effects. An Affliction will automatically expire after a certain amount of turns have passed and also have the option to trigger 'End of Turn' effects, meanwhile Passive Effects, as the name implies, don't expire nor have the capability to trigger 'End of Turn' effects.
The '+1 Attack Stage' effect is an example of an Affliction.

effect-overview.title: Effect Overview
effect-overview.content: In this tab you can change default Active Effect settings; important to note is for effects to properly work the 'Transfer Effect' option should be enabled, and the 'Effect Suspended' option should be disabled.

effect-details.title: Effect Details
effect-details.content: Meanwhile in the Details tab you can set more detailed options, such as the duration of the affliction, any End of Turn damage it should deal, and more.

effect-changes-1.title: Effect Changes [1/3]
effect-changes-1.content: And then we get to the meat of the Automation system, Effect Changes. As mentioned earlier, if you are familiar with the 'Rules Based Automation' system, you should find the functionality here very familiar, except instead of 'Rules' we now have 'Changes'. Changes can be anything from a simple stat change, to a more complex modifier on a roll, or even adding effect applications to attacks, or granting different items & effect! For full details please check the Automation Guide on the wiki.

effect-changes-2.title: Effect Changes [2/3]
effect-changes-2.content:
You can simply add changes with the 'Create Change' button, and change the type of change you wish to create with the dropdown.
For '+1 Attack Stage' you can see there is one change of type 'AE-Like', which allows for a simple stat change, in this case, +1 to the Attack Combat Stage.

effect-changes-3.title: Effect Changes [3/3]
effect-changes-3.content: A useful tool to know about is the 'Edit as JSON' button, this will open the JSON view of the change, allowing you to make more complex changes than the UI allows for, or to copy a change to another item.

data-inspector.title: Data Inspector
data-inspector.content:
The Data Inspector is a powerful tool that allows you to see the data of an Item, Actor or Chat Message.
You can open the Data Inspector either by selecting 'Open Data Inspector' from an Actor/Item sheet's toolbar dropdown menu or by right-clicking a Chat Message that has a Roll and selecting 'Open Data Inspector'

data-inspector-actor.title: Data Inspector - Actor
data-inspector-actor.content:
This is the Data Inspector for an Actor.
Here you can view all underlying data.
Useful for figuring out the path of an attribute, or which Rolloptions are currently applied to an Actor.
You can check usage & control details by hovering on the [?] icon in the toolbar.

data-inspector-chat.title: Data Inspector - Chat Roll
data-inspector-chat.content:
This is the Data Inspector for a Chat Message that contains a Roll.
Here you can see all Selectors that this roll checked against as well as all Roll Options that where applied from the User and possible Target.
On the right side you can see the modifiers to the roll itself.
In the case of a roll that targets multiple Actors, you can choose to view target-based details by selecting the appropriate target on the left.

misc.title: Miscellaneous & FAQ
misc.description: A collection of miscellaneous tips and tricks, as well as a FAQ!

welcome.title: Welcome to the Miscellaneous & FAQ Tour!
welcome.content: This tour is here to quickly show you a couple of buttons and features that you may have otherwise missed, as well as answer some frequently asked questions!

tooltips.title: Tooltips
tooltips.content:
Hovering over most things in the system will show you a tooltip with more information, this can be a great way to quickly get more information on a topic!
But not only that, if you pause for a moment, you will see a circle fill up, once it's filled up the tooltip will be 'locked in place', allowing you to interact with it, copying text, pressing buttons, or hovering over other elements which may have their own Tooltips!
You can also 'Middle Mouse Click' to instantly lock a tooltip in place!

exp-tracker.title: EXP & Venture Tracker
exp-tracker.content:
Over on the left hand-side controls, you can find some useful tools, like the EXP Tracker, which allows you to mark down any Circumstance Modifiers for your current Venture as they occur!
Besides this, you can also find the 'Training' tab here, which you can use for the 'Super Training' Camp Activity!

user-config.title: User Configuration
user-config.content:
A couple of features; like the EXP & Venture Tracker, make use of what characters are marked as PCs in Foundry.
Thus to get automation to fully function; players must set their PCs as their 'Player Character' in the Foundry User Configuration which they can access from down here, in the bottom left corner.

tutor-list.title: Tutor List
tutor-list.content:
Ever wonder what moves are tutorable to your Characters? Worry not, you can find a list of all tutorable moves in the 'Tutor List' Application!
It can be accessed from the Sidebar, or by using the 'Open Tutor List' button in the Biography tab of the Actor Sheet!

text-enrichers.title: Text Enrichers
text-enrichers.content:
PTR 2e supports a couple of custom Text Enrichers that can help you easily reference other data!

Unfamiliar with the term 'Text Enricher'? Text enrichers allow you to easily reference other types of data; for example, in a Journal Entry you can reference to another page, an actor, or even an item, by for example using @UUID[Compendium.ptr2e.core-effects.Item.burnconditioitem], which would create a clickable button for the Burn Affliction!

The following enrichers are added by the system:

  • @Trait[slug-of-trait] This makes a hoverable Trait link so you can quickly reference the Trait's info!
  • @Affliction[slug-of-affliction] This creates an item link much like @UUID without needing to know the full id of the affliction!
  • @Tick[number] Allows the quick creation of buttons that heal or deal Tick based Damage. You can also use @Tick[number pp] and @Tick[number shield] to deal/heal against PP and Shield Health respectively.

habitat-table.title: Habitat Tables
habitat-table.content: Dynamic Habitat Tables are generated automatically based on the 'habitat' field of Species in the Core Species Compendium. These tables can be used in Blueprint Generation to generate random Pokémon based on their habitat, or to quickly find a Pokémon of a specific habitat! Please note: these tables are 'virtual' tables, meaning you cannot use them in Macros the same way you would a regular table, they are, *generally*, only available for use in the Blueprint Generation system.

Ranges.self: Affects the character using this Action.
Ranges.blast: Choose one space within RIP 1, the Blast radius is a X.5m radius circle/sphere, centered on the selected space, where X is the value of the [Blast X] trait on this Action.
Ranges.cone:
Using the Foundry Cone tool, draw a cone from the center of the character token using this Action, with a length equal to the RIP plus the distance from the center of the character token to the edge of the token.

For example, a Medium creature w/ cone 3 would draw a cone 3.5m long, while a Large creature would draw one 4.0m long.
Ranges.line: A 1m wide line that is RIP long, starting from the center of the character token using this Action.
Ranges.wide-line: A wide line is similar to a regular line, except for its just as wide as the width of the character token using this Action, minimum 2m wide.
Ranges.emanation: An emanation is a RIP meter radius circle centered on the character token using this Action.
Ranges.field: Field targets every creature in the current combat.
Ranges.aura: An Aura is an Emanation that persists around the character token using this Action.
Ranges.allied-aura: An Allied Aura is an Aura that only affects allies.
Ranges.enemy-aura: An Enemy Aura is an Aura that only affects enemies.
